long_stay: Exchange programs between higher education institutions
Vocational training programs (provided the institution is accredited)
Language courses or preparatory courses (if mandatory prerequisite for main degree program)
qualifying_courses.notes: Non-formal training programs (short courses, language courses not part of a degree) typically do not qualify for a study residence permit.

types.name: National (D) Visa for Study Purposes
types.notes: Typically issued for the first year of studies for stays longer than 90 days.

booking_methods.description: Electronic application submission and initial registration.
External service provider for application submission and biometric data collection in most countries.
Direct appointment booking at embassies or consulates where VFS Global is not available.

steps.title: Get admitted to a Lithuanian higher education institution
University submits a 'mediation letter' through MIGRIS
steps.description: The mediation letter confirms your enrollment and is submitted by the university via the Lithuanian Migration Information System (MIGRIS), which assigns it a number.

steps.title: Fill out your visa application electronically in MIGRIS
steps.description: Complete the online application form and upload digital copies of all required documents.

steps.title: Book an appointment
steps.description: Schedule an appointment with the External Service Provider (e.g., VFS Global) or the Lithuanian embassy/consulate.

steps.title: Attend in person to submit biometrics and original documents
steps.description: Provide biometric data (fingerprints and photo) and submit physical copies of the required documents.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Issued within the last 10 years, valid for at least 3 months longer than the visa duration, with at least 2 blank pages.

required_documents.name: Mediation Letter
required_documents.details: Electronic mediation letter number from the Lithuanian educational institution, submitted via MIGRIS.

required_documents.name: Proof of Sufficient Financial Means
required_documents.details: Bank statements (covering the last 3 months), scholarship certificate, sponsorship affidavit, or income tax returns.

required_documents.name: Health Insurance
required_documents.details: Valid in Lithuania and all Schengen countries, with a minimum coverage of 30,000 EUR for a D visa.

required_documents.name: Completed Application Form
required_documents.details: Generated and completed electronically via MIGRIS, then printed and signed.

required_documents.name: Clean Criminal Record Certificate
required_documents.details: Required for Temporary Residence Permit (TRP) applications.

financial_requirements.reference_basis: 0.5 of the Minimum Monthly Wage (MMA) per month for living expenses

residence_permit.authority_full_name: Migration Department under the Ministry of the Interior
required_documents: Proof of sufficient funds (may require full MMA for some TRP types)
New mediation letter from educational institution (for renewal)
residence_permit.notes: Non-EU students must register their address with the Migration Department within 5 days of arrival. A Temporary Residence Permit (TRP) is usually issued for 1-2 years, up to 3 years for PhD students. Health insurance for TRP may require a minimum coverage of €6,000.

work_while_studying.legal_basis: Law on the Legal Status of Foreigners
restrictions: Bachelor's Year 1-2 students are limited to 20 hours/week (effective May 22, 2026).
Bachelor's Year 3+, Master's, and PhD students can work up to 40 hours/week.
The 20-hour limit does not apply to students who received a TRP before May 21, 2026.
Food delivery and ride-hailing platforms are required to track foreign students' working hours.
Universities will monitor student attendance and academic progress more strictly.
work_while_studying.notes: Rules are currently in flux due to legislative changes effective May 22, 2026. Students should verify current work hour restrictions on the Migration Department website.

required_documents: New mediation letter from the educational institution confirming enrollment and academic progress
permit_renewal.notes: Students can set up automatic email reminders in their MIGRIS account, which notify them 120 days and 60 days before their document expires.

post_study_work.legal_basis: Law on the Legal Status of Foreigners
requirements: Graduates of Lithuanian higher education institutions are exempt from the Employment Service decision on labor market suitability for 5 years after graduation.
Must notify the Migration Department within 10 working days of employment termination.
post_study_work.notes: The temporary residence permit issued or renewed for a student's final year of studies remains valid for 3 months after the expected end of their studies, providing a transition window.

official_sources.name: Migration Department under the Ministry of the Interior
official_sources.description: Main authority for visas, temporary residence permits, and legal requirements.

official_sources.name: MIGRIS (Lithuanian Migration Information System)
official_sources.description: Online application system for visas and residence permits.

official_sources.name: Study in Lithuania Portal
official_sources.description: Government/university-backed portal with study and visa overview for international students.

official_sources.name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Lithuania
official_sources.description: Provides general information on foreign policy and consular services.

official_sources.name: EU Immigration Portal
official_sources.description: EU-level overview of Lithuania's rules for non-EU students.

tips.title: Check Mediation Letter Status
tips.body: Ensure the university has officially uploaded the mediation letter to MIGRIS before booking your appointment.

tips.title: Calculate Funds Accurately
tips.body: Ensure bank statements show the total required amount for the entire duration of the permit (usually one year), using the current year's official minimum monthly wage (MMA).

tips.title: Verify Insurance Compliance
tips.body: Confirm that your health insurance policy specifically covers Lithuania and meets the minimum 30,000 EUR requirement for a D visa.

tips.title: Translate and Legalize Documents
tips.body: All documents not in Lithuanian or English must be translated and, in many cases, legalized or apostilled.

tips.title: Apply Early
tips.body: Submit your application as soon as you receive your acceptance letter to account for potential delays, especially during peak seasons.

tips.title: Set Renewal Reminders
tips.body: Utilize MIGRIS account features to set up automatic email reminders for permit renewal deadlines.

common_mistakes.consequence: Delays in visa/TRP processing or permit lapse.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Start the mediation letter and MIGRIS application as early as possible.
Always check the current minimum monthly salary on migracija.lt before calculating required funds.
Carefully review the exact, current document checklist in your MIGRIS application.
Ensure the university submits the mediation letter first and provides its number before you apply.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not declaring a place of residence
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Register your address with the Migration Department within 5 days of arrival (for non-EU students).

common_mistakes.mistake: Assuming old work-hour rules still apply
common_mistakes.consequence: Violation of permit conditions, potential deportation.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Verify current work hour restrictions on the Migration Department's website, especially for bachelor's students in their first two years.

common_mistakes.consequence: Loss of legal status in Lithuania.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Set up automatic email reminders in your MIGRIS account and apply within the specified window (1-4 months before expiry).
